title: PM Draft Packet-History Residue Split Authoring
objective: Convert the remaining live-looking 2026-04-18 draft packet JSON residue into separately executable follow-on packets instead of reopening the PM baseline/parser lane and the PM UI/read-surface lane as one broad packet-history rewrite.
dependency_note: After Packet 191 closed the remaining live-looking parent-root draft-publication handoff chain, the adjacent residue moved into the underlying 2026-04-18 packet-definition JSONs. Those files now split cleanly into two branches: a PM baseline/parser chain across `pm-schema-020d` through `pm-schema-020h`, and a PM UI/read-surface chain across `pm-schema-ui-002d` through `pm-schema-ui-002g`. This packet authors those follow-ons separately so the remaining residue stays bounded by implementation branch rather than reopened as one broad JSON rewrite.
authoring_scope: Create a ready execution packet for the PM baseline/parser draft packet JSON family across `pm-schema-020d` through `pm-schema-020h`.
Create a ready execution packet for the PM UI/read-surface draft packet JSON family across `pm-schema-ui-002d` through `pm-schema-ui-002g`.
Update repo-owned status routing so the split is explicit and the next work can proceed as separate residue slices.
forbidden_scope: editing the historical target PM baseline/parser or PM UI/read-surface packet JSON files themselves in this authoring packet
success_criteria: A ready packet spec exists for the PM baseline/parser draft packet JSON family.
A ready packet spec exists for the PM UI/read-surface draft packet JSON family.
PROJECT_STATUS.md records the split so the next lane is explicit and bounded.
next_action: Execute Packet 193 or Packet 194 next. If both are needed, keep them as separate residue slices.
